title: Create equations to solve for missing angles
description: Use understanding of vertical, complementary, and supplementary angles to set up an appropriate equation to solve for a missing angle measure.
topic_path: Using equations to solve for unknown angles
Create equations to solve for missing angles

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{35^\circ} +\blueD{5x^\circ}= 90^\circ$

options.alt: A right angle is divided into 2 equal parts, labeled as 35 degrees and 5 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{(5x + 30)^\circ} +\blueD{5x^\circ}= 90^\circ$

options.alt: A line divides a right angle into two parts labeled as parenthesis open 5 times x plus 30 parenthesis closed degrees and 5 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{x^\circ} +\blueD{(6x - 15)^\circ}= 90^\circ$

options.alt: A line divides a right angle into two parts labeled as x degrees and parenthesis open 6 times x minus 15 parenthesis closed degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{7x^\circ} +\blueD{11x ^\circ}= 90^\circ$

options.alt: A ray divides a right angle into two parts labeled 7 X degrees and 11 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{x^\circ} +\blueD{(4x-85) ^\circ}= 90^\circ$

options.alt: A ray divides a straight right angle into two parts labeled as x degrees and parenthesis open 4 times x minus 85 parenthesis closed degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{3x^\circ} +\blueD{(8x-20) ^\circ}= 90^\circ$

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight angle into three parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as 3 X degrees, parenthesis open 8 times x minus 20 parenthesis closed degrees, and a right angle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are complementary angles because they combine to form a right angle.
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{2x ^\circ} + \goldD{30^\circ} = 90^\circ$

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight angle into three parts labeled as 30 degrees, 2 X degrees and a right angle, from left to right, respectively.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are supplementary angles because they combine to form a straight line.
Supplementary angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{4x^\circ} +\blueD{5x^\circ}= 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked and labeled as 4 X degrees. Another angle adjacent to this is marked and labeled as 5 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are supplementary angles because they combine to form a straight line.
Supplementary angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{(2x + 15)^\circ} +\blueD{65^\circ}= 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two lines intersecting each other, are creating supplementary angles of 65 degrees and 2X plus 15 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are supplementary angles because they combine to form a straight line.
Supplementary angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{(3x + 12)^\circ} +\blueD{x^\circ}= 180^\circ$

options.alt: A ray divides a straight angle into two parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked and labeled as parenthesis open 3 X plus 12 parenthesis closed degrees and X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are supplementary angles because they combine to form a straight line.
Supplementary angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{5x^\circ} +\blueD{13x^\circ}= 180^\circ$

options.alt: A ray divides a straight horizontal line into two angles labeled 5 X degrees and 13 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The three labeled angles are supplementary angles because they combine to form a straight line.
Supplementary angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{3x^\circ} +\blueD{80^\circ} +\maroonD{2x^\circ}= 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight horizontal in three diffrent angles labeled 3 X degrees, 80 degrees, and 2 X degrees, from left to right, respectively.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The three labeled angles are supplementary angles because they combine to form a straight line.
Supplementary angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.  So, the labeled angles have a sum of $180^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{6x^\circ} +\blueD{12x^\circ} + \maroonD{90^\circ}= 180^\circ$

options.alt: A straight horizontal line is perpendicular to a straight vertical ray. One of the two angles made by the line and the ray is divided into unequal parts by another ray. The measurements of these unequal angles are 6 X degrees and 12 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are vertical angles because they are opposite each other where two lines cross.
Vertical angles are congruent.   So, the labeled angle measures are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{60^\circ} = \blueD{(x+45)^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersecting each other, are creating opposite angles of 60 degrees and X plus 45 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are vertical angles because they are opposite each other where two lines cross.
Vertical angles are congruent.   So, the labeled angle measures are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{105^\circ} = \blueD{(5x-70)^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as parenthesis open 5 times x minus 70 parenthesis closed degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked as 105 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are vertical angles because they are opposite each other where two lines cross.
Vertical angles are congruent.   So, the labeled angle measures are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{60^\circ} = \blueD{(4x-20)^\circ}$

options.alt: Two slant lines and a ray intersect at a single point. Two lines are create angles labeled as 60 degrees and 4 X minus 20 degrees opposite to each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two labeled angles are vertical angles because they are opposite each other where two lines cross.
Vertical angles are equivalent.   So, the labeled angles are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{3x^\circ} = \blueD{30^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 30 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is labeled as parenthesis open 3 times x parenthesis closed degrees. The angle between these two is divided by a ray into two parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The labeled angles are vertical angles because they are opposite each other where two lines cross.
Vertical angles are congruent.   So, the labeled angle measures are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{(10x+10)^\circ} = \blueD{110^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 110 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked and labeled as parenthesis open 10 times x plus 10 parenthesis closed degrees. The angle between these two is divided by a ray into two parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\goldD{2x^\circ}$ and  $\maroonD{4x^\circ}$ combine to form an angle that is opposite the $\blueD{150^\circ}$ angle where two lines meet.

This means $\goldD{2x^\circ}$ combined with  $\maroonD{4x^\circ}$ and $\blueD{150^\circ}$ are vertical angles.
Vertical angles are equivalent.   So, the labeled angles are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{2x^\circ} + \maroonD{4x^\circ}= \blueD{150^\circ} $

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 150 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is divided by a ray into two parts labeled 2 X degrees and 4 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can be used to solve for $x$ in the following diagram?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The labeled angles are vertical angles because they are opposite each other where two lines cross.
Vertical angles are equivalent.   So, the labeled angles are equivalent.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldD{10x^\circ} = \blueD{150^\circ} $

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is labeled as 150 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is labeled as 10 X degrees.
